label: About
title: About Glaze Weather Plinth
short_blurb: A daily generated front page where the image is the interface.
body: Daily Frontpage is a daily generated interactive front page. Each edition starts from recent saved signals and source research, becomes a new image-led scene, and stays explorable in the archive. The image is the interface: visible marks, objects, and surfaces in the scene open the real sources that shaped the day.

This iteration began with 95 recent saved-signal files and 71 captured links, then narrowed the page to 9 source windows around @digitlartifacts: gorgeous ceramics from artist hedy yang and Anchor image material 1, plus 7 more. A source image from pbs.twimg.com helped set the palette, contrast, edge behavior, and gesture. The scene became Glaze Weather Plinth: Glaze Weather Plinth: a product-photo rupture on an oblique product table, one oversized handmade ceramic body cropped hard by two image edges, with two smaller rounded masses behind it. Warm white plinth, peach clay, milky blue, cobalt, jade, tomato glaze, soft black shadow. After the image was made, a plate-reading pass mapped 9 visible regions and the mask audit tightened them against cropped red-orange glaze shoulder on the main vessel, large uneven blue glaze aperture on the right wall, and rear vessel peach seam below blue cap.

typography.rationale: Bricolage Grotesque and Space Grotesk fit record, arcade, and listening-room editions while Fraunces keeps source captions tactile.
